data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me plan the migration from xUnit v2 to xUnit v3:

1. **TestProject.csproj changes:**
   - Replace `xunit` (v2) with `xunit.v3` (v3)
   - Remove `xunit.runner.visualstudio` - v3 uses `xunit.runner.v3` or the projects become self-executable
   - Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` - not needed for v3 (v3 tests are standalone executables)
   - Replace `Xunit.Combinatorial` with the v3-compatible version
   - Also swap out `Xunit.StaFact` for its v3 equivalent
   - Set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` since v3 test projects compile as executables
   - Keep the SDK as `Microsoft.NET.Sdk`

I need to verify the exact v3 package names - `xunit.v3` is the main package, and I should check what the runner package is called in v3. The core API remains largely the same—`[Fact]` and `[Theory]` attributes still work, and the `Assert` class is unchanged. For the extension packages, I need to check compatibility: `Xunit.Combinatorial` v2.0+ supports v3, and `Xunit.StaFact` also has a v2.0+ version for v3 compatibility.

For the migration itself, the main steps are updating the package references (moving from `xunit` 2.9.3 to `xunit.v3`, removing the VS runner and test SDK packages, and getting v3-compatible versions of the extensions), changing the project file to include `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>`, and potentially adjusting namespaces in the code if anything shifted.

The `global.json` configuration needs attention too—xUnit v3 has its own built-in runner and uses Microsoft.Testing.Platform by default, so the VSTest runner setting might need to be removed or updated. I should verify the latest package versions for the Combinatorial and StaFact extensions to ensure they support v3. v3-compatible versions. For the global.json, I need to consider that xUnit v3 projects are standalone executables, and while VSTest can still work, Microsoft.Testing.Platform is the recommended approach. Let me verify what configuration is needed when running `dotnet test` with v3. aFact). This is fine.

Let me work through the migration steps: I need to check the latest versions, update the project file, modify the code if necessary, adjust the global.json settings, then build and test. For xUnit v3, the main changes are swapping `xunit` for `xunit.v3`, removing `xunit.runner.visualstudio` and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, adding `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` to the project, and keeping most of the existing test attributes and assertions since they remain compatible.

I'm trying to figure out the right v3-compatible package for combinatorial data attributes and whether I need to adjust the using statements. Let me just start making the changes to the project file and see what restores and builds successfully. Let me just try building the project as-is to see what errors come up, then work through the namespace issues. Since we're on Linux and the target framework is `net8.0-windows`, that might be a blocker—I should check what .NET SDK is available first.

Good, we have .NET 9, 10, and 11 preview. Since `net8.0-windows` is used and we're on Linux, we may need to change the TFM. But let me first try to build the baseline to understand the situation.

Actually, `net8.0-windows` won't work on Linux without the Windows targeting pack. Let me try the baseline build and see what happens. If it fails, I'll still proceed with the migration - the TFM might need updating too.

Let me try the baseline restore first.

Good, I have all the version info. Now let me apply the migration changes.

**xUnit v3 migration plan:**
- `xunit` → `xunit.v3` 3.0.0
- Remove `xunit.runner.visualstudio` & `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (v3 tests are standalone executables)
- `Xunit.Combinatorial` → 2.0.24 (v3-compatible)
- `Xunit.StaFact` → 3.0.13 (v3-compatible)
- Add `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>`
- Update `global.json`

data.reasoningText:
The `Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App` framework reference is coming from one of the packages (likely `Xunit.StaFact` 3.0.13 which might pull in WPF/WinForms dependencies). Let me check if this is a Linux issue.

Actually, `net8.0-windows` with the old v2 packages built fine. The issue is that one of the new packages (probably StaFact 3.0.13) is pulling in `Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App`. Let me check what dependencies the new StaFact has.

Actually wait - the original project built fine with `net8.0-windows` on Linux. 

The real problem is probably that the .NET 8 SDK on Linux doesn't include the `Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App` targeting pack, and one of the newer packages has a transitive dependency that requires it, whereas the older v2 packages didn't.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<package xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/packaging/2013/05/nuspec.xsd">
  <metadata>
    <id>Xunit.StaFact</id>
    <version>3.0.13</version>
    <authors>Andrew Arnott</authors>
    <license type="expression">MS-PL</license>
    <licenseUrl>https://licenses.nuget.org/MS-PL</licenseUrl>
    <readme>README.md</readme>
    <projectUrl>https://aarnott.github.io/Xunit.StaFact/</projectUrl>
    <description>Run your xunit-based tests on an STA thread with the WPF Dispatcher, a WinForms SynchronizationContext, or even a cross-platform generic UI thread emulation with a SynchronizationContext that keeps code running on a "main thread" for that test.

Simply use [WpfFact], [WinFormsFact], [StaFact] or the cross-platform [UIFact] on your test method to run your test under conditions that most closely match the main thread in your application.

Theory variants of these attributes allow for parameterized testing. Check out the xunit.combinatorial nuget package for pairwise or combinatorial testing with theories.</description>
    <releaseNotes>https://github.com/AArnott/Xunit.StaFact/releases/tag/v3.0.13</releaseNotes>
    <copyright>© Andrew Arnott. All rights reserved.</copyright>
    <tags>STA xunit testing unit WPF WinForms testing</tags>
    <repository type="git" url="https://github.com/AArnott/Xunit.StaFact" branch="refs/heads/main" commit="9fbe278efe7bafd2ce7618b6d6ae0baaa08cce4d" />
    <dependencies>
      <group targetFramework=".NETFramework4.7.2">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="3.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework="net8.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="3.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework="net8.0-macos14.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="3.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework="net8.0-windows7.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="3.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework=".NETStandard2.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="3.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
    </dependencies>
    <frameworkReferences>
      <group targetFramework="net8.0" />
      <group targetFramework="net8.0-macos14.0" />
      <group targetFramework="net8.0-windows7.0">
        <frameworkReference name="Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework=".NETFramework4.7.2" />
      <group targetFramework=".NETStandard2.0" />
    </frameworkReferences>
  </metadata>
</package>
<exited with exit code 0>

data.content: The build failure is expected on Linux — `Xunit.StaFact` 3.0.13 adds a `Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App` framework reference for `net8.0-windows`. Let me verify the code compiles correctly by temporarily using `net8.0`:
data.reasoningText:
The `Xunit.StaFact` 3.0.13 has a framework reference `Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App` for `net8.0-windows7.0`. Since our project targets `net8.0-windows`, NuGet resolves to the `net8.0-windows7.0` TFM group, which pulls in `Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App`. This framework isn't available on Linux.

The original project with StaFact 1.1.11 likely didn't have this framework reference.

Since we're on Linux and can't install the Windows Desktop targeting pack, the build will fail with this TFM. However, the migration itself is correct - this would build on Windows.


I could temporarily switch to `net8.0` to verify the code compiles correctly, then switch back to `net8.0-windows` since that's what the project actually needs for STA threading and WPF dispatcher support. The build failure is just an environment limitation on Linux, not a problem with the migration itself.

data.content:
Migration complete. Here's a summary of the changes:

**`TestProject.csproj`:**
- Added `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` — xUnit v3 tests are standalone executables
- `xunit` 2.9.3 → `xunit.v3` 3.0.0
- Removed `xunit.runner.visualstudio` and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` — no longer needed in v3
- `Xunit.Combinatorial` 1.6.24 → 2.0.24 (v3-compatible)
- `Xunit.StaFact` 1.1.11 → 3.0.13 (v3-compatible)

**`global.json`:**
- Changed test runner from `VSTest` to `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` (v3's native runner)

**`CombinatorialTests.cs`:** No code changes needed — `[Theory]`, `[CombinatorialData]`, `[StaFact]`, and `Assert` APIs are source-compatible.

> **Note:** The full build with `net8.0-windows` requires a Windows environment (StaFact v3 pulls in `Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App`). I verified the code compiles cleanly against `net8.0` on this Linux host.
